intro: Launaea stenocephala, also known as the East African lettuce, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its edible leaves and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
